journal_entry.description: Receipt of security deposit for 1-year lease of building space
journal_entry.rationale: Security deposit received is recorded as a liability (refundable deposit) because Mondelez has an obligation to return it to the tenant at lease end, subject to any damages or unpaid rent. The deposit does not represent earned revenue. Cash increases (debit asset), and a liability is created (credit liability). No revenue is recognized at this time because the deposit is refundable and not payment for rent. The transaction states the deposit was received upon signing, so this entry records only the deposit receipt. No sales tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply.

output.reason: Mondelez receives $25,000 cash security deposit. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset. This creates 1 debit line: Asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) on the credit side, but we only classify debits here.

debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez receives $25,000 cash security deposit. Cash is an asset, and receiving cash increases the asset. This creates 1 debit line: Asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) on the credit side, but we only classify debits here.

output.reason: The transaction involves receiving cash (security deposit). On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) = liability increase. The cash received is the debit side, not classified here.

credit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves receiving cash (security deposit). On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit obligation) = liability increase. The cash received is the debit side, not classified here.

output.reason: Entry correctly records receipt of security deposit. Balance verified: $25,000 debit = $25,000 credit. Security deposit is properly classified as a liability (Security Deposits Payable) since it is refundable to the tenant at lease end, not earned revenue. Cash debit correctly reflects asset increase. No tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply - they are refundable amounts held in trust. Amounts match transaction text exactly. All accounts and directionality correct under IFRS.
